status: synthetic structural-identifiability theorem; not material validation

exact_invariances.gap_carrier_translation: R depends on Eg0 and Delta_carrier only through Eg0+Delta_carrier
exact_invariances.amplitude_thickness_product: R depends on A and d only through A*d

unmarked_dense_spectrum.interpretation: dense noise-free sampling cannot recover the two exact null directions

external_constraint_ranks.interpretation: one external constraint removes one null direction; both are required to identify all three remaining combinations

nontranslational_carrier_marker.physical_status: generic identifiability marker; not the Dingrong free-carrier model
nontranslational_carrier_marker.remaining_null_direction: ln absorption amplitude versus ln effective thickness
nontranslational_carrier_marker.interpretation: independently calibrated carrier-dependent spectral structure can break the Eg/carrier translation null, but Beer-Lambert amplitude/thickness degeneracy remains

authorized_conclusions: A dense single-state response spectrum identifies at most three combinations of the five declared parameters when carrier effects are a uniform translation.
No increase in signal-to-noise or spectral point count can remove exact forward-model invariances.
Independent carrier-state information and effective-thickness information are both required for latent-gap recovery under this model.
A nontranslational carrier-dependent spectral feature can break the gap/carrier translation degeneracy if its shape and calibration are retained.
unauthorized_conclusions: The controlled Gaussian and power-law spectrum is not a complete microscopic HgCdTe model.
The generic carrier marker is not a physical free-carrier absorption law.
Effective thickness is not assumed equal to physical thickness.
The structural rank theorem does not determine practical uncertainty after external constraints are applied.
Spatial correlations between carrier state and local gap are outside the theorem.
